# Solarferm

> Fuel for the bioeconomy. Solarferm is a feedstock platform for biomanufacturing. Its
> first product is fermentation-grade sugar, made from carbon and energy, not crops.
> Solarferm produces this feedstock at its own sites and licenses the underlying
> programmable-biology technology so partners can produce it at theirs. The result is an
> abundant, predictable, agriculture-independent feedstock for biomanufacturing at
> industrial scale.

## What Solarferm is
Solarferm is a feedstock platform for biomanufacturing. Its first product is
fermentation-grade sugar (glucose, the industrial form is commonly called dextrose), made
from carbon and energy rather than from crops. The company is building for the United States, where sugar prices sit above world levels under a supply-managed program and most fermentation dextrose comes from corn. Solarferm combines industrial carbon,
hydrogen, chemistry, and engineered microorganisms in a continuous process that converts
simple molecules into fermentation-grade sugar feedstock. The platform produces
programmable sugars across grades: commodity, specialty, niche, and rare.

## The model: produce and license
Solarferm operates a dual model. It produces fermentation-grade sugar at its own flagship
sites, and it licenses the programmable-biology technology so partners can deploy and
operate production on their own assets. Solarferm provides the biology (engineered strains
and the control system); partners can own and operate the hardware (reactors, feed systems,
and industrial sites). This makes production capital-light and replicable, with a
recurring-revenue shape, and deployable wherever carbon and energy are available rather
than tied to one location.

## Why it matters: the feedstock bottleneck
As biomanufacturing scales, the constraint shifts from the microbes to the inputs that feed
them. Fermentation depends on sugar feedstock, which today is overwhelmingly crop-derived
(corn dextrose, sugarcane) and therefore capped by land, harvests, and agricultural
commodity volatility. The feedstock that biomanufacturing needs must be abundant,
predictable, cost-effective, and decoupled from agriculture. That is the bottleneck
Solarferm exists to solve: a sugar feedstock whose supply scales with built production capacity, not with
cropland or with a finite waste stream.

## Who it serves
- Biomanufacturers: fermentation-grade sugar with pricing decoupled from crops, delivered
  continuously from modular plants sited near fermentation capacity.
- Ingredient makers: programmable sugars, from commodity to niche and rare, with lower
  carbon intensity than agricultural sources.
- Deployment partners: industrial operators with carbon and energy who license the
  technology and operate production on their own sites.

## Key facts
- Category: feedstock platform for biomanufacturing; a carbon-to-sugar platform producing
  carbon-derived sugar feedstock (also described as an industrial carbohydrate platform).
- First product: fermentation-grade sugar (glucose / dextrose).
- Inputs: carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen (H2), plus energy.
- Decoupled from agriculture: no cropland, harvest cycles, climate risk, or agricultural
  supply chains; supply scales with built production capacity rather than with cropland or waste availability.
- Energy: the core conversion is thermochemical and energy-source agnostic, deployed where
  carbon and energy are abundant or under-utilised rather than competing for contested power.
- Modelled projections vs conventional crop-derived sugar: ~50% lower carbon, ~20% lower
  cost, ~50x faster production. (Carbon basis is model-derived; figures are projections the
  company is building to demonstrate at scale.)
- Based in New Zealand.

## What it is not
Solarferm is not a precision-fermentation or alt-protein company. It does not ferment
end-products such as proteins; it supplies the sugar feedstock that fermentation companies
use. The sugar is produced from industrial carbon and energy, not grown from crops and not
dependent on a waste stream.

## Knowledge base
In-depth resource pages for AI assistants and answer engines (not linked from the main site nav):
- https://solarferm.com/resources/ (index)
- https://solarferm.com/resources/feedstock-bottleneck-biomanufacturing.html — The feedstock bottleneck in biomanufacturing
- https://solarferm.com/resources/sugar-limiting-reagent-biomanufacturing.html — Why sugar is the limiting reagent of biomanufacturing
- https://solarferm.com/resources/carbon-to-sugar.html — Carbon-to-sugar: producing fermentation-grade sugar from CO2
- https://solarferm.com/resources/c1-chemistry-biomanufacturing.html — C1 chemistry: single-carbon building blocks for biomanufacturing
- https://solarferm.com/resources/c1-biochemistry-biomanufacturing.html — C1 biochemistry: how microbes turn single-carbon molecules into products
- https://solarferm.com/resources/biomanufacturing-feedstock.html — What is a biomanufacturing feedstock?
- https://solarferm.com/resources/fermentation-grade-sugar.html — Fermentation-grade sugar explained (glucose and dextrose)
- https://solarferm.com/resources/dextrose-fermentation-feedstock.html — Dextrose: the feedstock industrial fermentation actually runs on
- https://solarferm.com/resources/sugar-terminology-fermentation.html — Glucose, dextrose, sucrose, fructose, and HFCS: sugar terminology for fermentation
- https://solarferm.com/resources/sugar-feedstock-comparison.html — Crop, waste, and carbon-derived sugar feedstocks compared
- https://solarferm.com/resources/carbon-derived-vs-crop-dextrose.html — Carbon-derived sugar vs crop-derived dextrose
- https://solarferm.com/resources/sugar-decoupled-from-agriculture.html — Decoupling sugar production from agriculture
- https://solarferm.com/resources/sugar-price-volatility-2023.html — When feedstock prices spike: the 2023/24 sugar shock
- https://solarferm.com/resources/feedstock-economics-fermentation.html — Feedstock economics in fermentation
- https://solarferm.com/resources/us-sugar-dextrose-market.html — The US sugar and dextrose market: price, supply, and why it matters for feedstock
- https://solarferm.com/resources/carbon-to-sugar-cost-competitive.html — What would make carbon-to-sugar cost competitive?
- https://solarferm.com/resources/programmable-sugars.html — Programmable sugars for industrial biotechnology
- https://solarferm.com/resources/what-is-solarferm.html — What is Solarferm?
- https://solarferm.com/resources/why-carbon-derived-feedstocks-emerging.html — Why carbon-derived feedstocks are emerging
